title: From model to agent: Equipping the Responses API with a computer environment
optimized_headline: Transforming the Responses API: How a Computer Environment Enhances Performance
raw_excerpt: How OpenAI built an agent runtime using the Responses API, shell tool, and hosted containers to run secure, scalable agents with files, tools, and state.
raw_body: How OpenAI built an agent runtime using the Responses API, shell tool, and hosted containers to run secure, scalable agents with files, tools, and state.
speedrun: OpenAI has developed an agent runtime that leverages the Responses API, shell tools, and hosted containers. This setup allows for secure and scalable agents to operate with files, tools, and state management. By integrating these components, OpenAI enhances the capabilities of AI systems to perform complex tasks more efficiently. This advancement is significant as it opens up new possibilities for AI applications in various domains.
why_it_matters: Developers can now create more capable AI agents, improving user interactions and task automation. This could lead to faster, more effective solutions for businesses.
This development indicates a shift towards more integrated AI systems that can manage complex tasks, reflecting a broader trend in AI towards enhanced functionality and adaptability.

lenses.eli12: OpenAI has made it easier for AI to handle tasks by creating a system that combines different tools and environments. Imagine this as giving an AI a toolbox and a workspace to build things more effectively. This matters because it means AI can help us solve problems faster and with more precision in our daily lives.
lenses.pm: For product managers and founders, this new agent runtime means more powerful AI tools that can handle complex tasks autonomously. It addresses user needs for efficiency and versatility while potentially lowering development costs. This could lead to quicker deployment of features that enhance user engagement and satisfaction.
lenses.engineer: The agent runtime utilizes the Responses API alongside shell tools and hosted containers, enabling a secure environment for running scalable agents. This architecture supports state management and file handling, enhancing the agents' capabilities. However, the article does not detail specific benchmarks or performance metrics, which would be critical for evaluating its effectiveness.
